cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The task is to figure out which answer is correct based on the given text. The question is asking what the text suggests, so I need to look for clues in the passage that align with one of the options. First, let me recall the content of the text. The passage is about Zhao Wenxuan, a Taiwanese film star. It mentions that he got his start in acting by chance when he applied for a role in the film "Wedding Feast." The director, Li An, chose him, and the film was a hit. It won awards like the German Golden Bear and was part of the Oscar competition. It was also the first Taiwan film allowed in the U.S. The text states that because of "Wedding Feast," Zhao became popular in Taiwan, Hong Kong, and Southeast Asia. Now, looking at the options: A) "Wedding Feast" is just an ordinary film – This seems unlikely because the text highlights its success, awards, and impact. B) The film "Wedding Feast" disappointed people greatly – No, the passage says the opposite; it was a hit and won awards. C) Zhao Wenxuan became a famous movie star because of the film "Wedding Feast" – This matches the text. The passage directly says that due to this film, he became popular in several regions. D) A lot of Taiwan films are shown in the U.S. every year – The text says "Wedding Feast" was the first Taiwan film permitted to be shown in the U.S., so this option might be incorrect because it's about "a lot" of films, not just one. I need to check if the text actually says that he became popular because of "Wedding Feast."
